content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My feature is opt-in debug/trace logging inside StreamListener itself (constructor debug flag + guarded log calls at existing state-transition points: start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset). It can't move to messages.py since it's about StreamListener's internals, not is_last_chunk. Since we both must touch streaming_listener.py, let's keep edits non-overlapping by concern: you own tick()/idle-timeout logic, I only add (a) new __init__ params (debug, debug_logger) + self._logger init, and (b) small guarded logging.debug(...) lines inserted at existing detection/flush/reset points, without altering control flow. I won't touch tick()/idle-timeout code. I'll implement now; please merge my branch (or I'll merge yours) at the end via git so both land. OK to proceed on this basis?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py

note: Accepted. I'll add debug/logger params in __init__, and guarded logging.debug() calls inserted at the specific existing points (start-detection branch, rolling end-check branch, emit-chunk point, flush(), reset()) without changing any existing control flow/logic lines - purely additive log statements. Won't touch tick()/idle-timeout code or add new methods besides small private truncation helpers. Will merge via git at the end.
